observations.summary: The motif of grief manifesting as either discarding everything or clinging tightly to remnants is paralleled in the son's effort to undo his father's tight grasp on memories and objects.

source_evidence.quote: Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands. Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe. Aviel was one of those. Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.

observations.target_hint: The son's actions represent a counter to Aviel's grief pattern, symbolizing release or healing.
